title: cytisus hirsutus var. perhirsutus
intro: Cytisus hirsutus var. perhirsutus, also known as Scotch broom, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to treat digestive issues, lower blood pressure, and act as an antiseptic. Cytisus hirsutus var. perhirsutus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its seeds have been used to make a coffee substitute, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
